article.headline: Wærenskjold wins fastest ever Tour de France stage in frenzied sprint to Nevers
article.excerpt_note_pt: Este é o trecho inicial da matéria, reproduzido palavra por palavra, sem modificações. A matéria completa está no link da fonte.
paragraphs: The records keep tumbling in the 2026 Tour de France. After race leader Tadej Pogacar shattered the record for the fastest climb of the Col du Tourmalet, Norwegian sprinter Søren Wærenskjold won the fastest-ever road stage, in a frenzied sprint into Nevers.

Pogacar revealed his stage had not been entirely straightforward. “I ran over a loose bottle with my front wheel and almost crashed,” he said. “I completely shat my pants there. Luckily, I managed to keep my handlebars upright. It’s nice to have days like this, but you still have to keep your focus throughout the stage.”
Wærenskjold took the 11th stage five days after his teammate Torsten Træen crashed out of the Tour in the yellow jersey, on the Pyrenean stage to Gavarnie-Gèdre.
His win came in a stage raced at an average speed of 50.9km/h, in an edition of the Tour that has also been one of the hottest.

“It means everything,” the Norwegian rider said of his first Tour success. “It’s my biggest win so far. Sometimes, I have good confidence and believe in myself. Sometimes, I feel tired and like it’s impossible to win here. I felt really bad at the start, but then I felt better in the final, with adrenaline and everything.”

The afternoon following France’s shock defeat to Spain at the World Cup, the sight of Julian Alaphilippe in the day’s four-man breakaway was a reminder of happier times. But that only led to renewed disappointment for home fans when the former world road race champion, who became a national hero when he held the yellow jersey for 14 days in the 2019 Tour, was dropped on the Côte-de-Chevannes, 38km from the finish. That may prove to have been 34-year-old Alaphilippe’s last hurrah in the Tour de France, although you can never say Nevers again.

vocabulary.meaning_en: falling one after another; being broken repeatedly
vocabulary.meaning_pt: caindo, sendo quebrados (um atrás do outro)
vocabulary.example: The records keep tumbling in the 2026 Tour de France.

vocabulary.meaning_en: broke completely (here: broke a record decisively)
vocabulary.example: After race leader Tadej Pogacar shattered the record for the fastest climb of the Col du Tourmalet
Norwegian sprinter Søren Wærenskjold won the fastest-ever road stage, in a frenzied sprint into Nevers.
Pogacar revealed his stage had not been entirely straightforward.

vocabulary.meaning_en: was forced to leave a race/competition after a crash
vocabulary.meaning_pt: saiu da competição por causa de uma queda
vocabulary.example: his teammate Torsten Træen crashed out of the Tour in the yellow jersey

vocabulary.meaning_en: a small group that rides/runs ahead of the main group
vocabulary.meaning_pt: grupo de fuga (à frente do pelotão)
vocabulary.example: the sight of Julian Alaphilippe in the day’s four-man breakaway was a reminder of happier times.

vocabulary.meaning_en: was left behind by the leading group
vocabulary.meaning_pt: foi deixado para trás (num grupo esportivo)
vocabulary.example: was a reminder of happier times. But that only led to renewed disappointment for home fans when the former world road race champion, who became a national hero when he held the yellow jersey for 14 days in the 2019 Tour, was dropped on the Côte-de-Chevannes, 38km from the finish.

vocabulary.meaning_en: a final moment of glory or celebration
vocabulary.meaning_pt: um último grande momento de glória
vocabulary.example: That may prove to have been 34-year-old Alaphilippe’s last hurrah in the Tour de France, although you can never say Nevers again.

reading.warmup_pt: Antes de ler: você já assistiu a uma corrida de ciclismo? Enquanto lê, repare em quem venceu a etapa e por que o dia foi especial.

questions.q: Who won stage 11 of the 2026 Tour de France?
questions.explain_pt: “Norwegian sprinter Søren Wærenskjold won the fastest-ever road stage, in a frenzied sprint into Nevers.”

questions.q: Tadej Pogacar actually crashed during the stage.
questions.explain_pt: Falso: ele disse “I ran over a loose bottle … and almost crashed” — ou seja, quase caiu, mas conseguiu manter o controle (“managed to keep my handlebars upright”).

questions.q: What made this stage's average speed notable?
options: It was the slowest stage of the Tour
It was raced at 50.9km/h, in one of the hottest editions
questions.explain_pt: “His win came in a stage raced at an average speed of 50.9km/h, in an edition of the Tour that has also been one of the hottest.”

questions.q: How long after his teammate's crash did Wærenskjold win the stage?
questions.explain_pt: “Wærenskjold took the 11th stage five days after his teammate Torsten Træen crashed out of the Tour in the yellow jersey.”

questions.q: Julian Alaphilippe once held the yellow jersey for 14 days in the 2019 Tour.
questions.explain_pt: Verdadeiro — o texto descreve Alaphilippe como “the former world road race champion, who became a national hero when he held the yellow jersey for 14 days in the 2019 Tour”.

listening.intro_pt: Ouça a notícia inteira SEM ler o texto (use 0.75x se precisar). Depois responda às perguntas e faça o ditado.

questions.q: What did Pogacar almost hit with his front wheel?
questions.explain_pt: “I ran over a loose bottle with my front wheel and almost crashed.”

questions.q: What was the average speed of the stage?
questions.explain_pt: “…a stage raced at an average speed of 50.9km/h…”

questions.q: How many riders were in the day's breakaway?
questions.explain_pt: “…the sight of Julian Alaphilippe in the day's four-man breakaway…”

writing.title: Contando a notícia para um amigo ciclista
writing.task_pt: Imagine que um amigo que ama ciclismo ainda não viu a notícia da etapa 11 do Tour de France. Escreva uma mensagem em inglês contando o que aconteceu. Use pelo menos DUAS informações reais da notícia e tente usar uma oração relativa (ex.: “the rider who…”, “a stage that…”).
writing.prompt_en: A friend who loves cycling hasn't seen today's news yet. Write a short message telling them what happened in stage 11 of the Tour de France. Use at least two facts from the article.
checklist_pt: Usei pelo menos duas informações reais da notícia (nome do vencedor, velocidade média, ou o quase-acidente de Pogacar).
Contei a notícia numa ordem clara (o que aconteceu → detalhes).
Tentei usar uma oração relativa (with 'who' or 'that').
Revisei os verbos no passado antes de enviar.
writing.model_answer: Hey! Big news from the Tour de France today. Søren Wærenskjold, who is a Norwegian sprinter, won stage 11 in a frenzied sprint to Nevers. It was the fastest road stage ever, raced at an average speed of 50.9km/h! Pogacar almost crashed after hitting a loose bottle, but he stayed upright. Crazy stage!
writing.model_note_pt: Resposta-modelo escrita por nós (NÃO faz parte da notícia). Use como referência de estrutura e tom; não copie.

His win came in a stage raced at an average speed
Wærenskjold took the 11th stage five days after his teammate
That may prove to have been 34-year-old Alaphilippe’s last

questions.q_en: Summarize what happened in stage 11 of the Tour de France, in your own words.
questions.support_pt: Estruture: Who won? → What was special about the stage? → What happened to Pogacar? Fale por 40–60 segundos.

questions.q_en: Pogacar almost crashed but kept racing hard. Why do you think athletes take risks like this?
Do you follow any sport closely? Describe a memorable moment, using a relative clause (e.g., “a player who…”).
questions.support_pt: Tente usar “who” ou “that” para descrever uma pessoa ou coisa.

speaking.tip_pt: Grave suas respostas no celular e ouça depois. Tente usar uma oração relativa (“who”/“that”) pelo menos uma vez.

grammar.title: Orações relativas básicas — “who” e “that”
grammar.point_pt:
Uma oração relativa dá mais informação sobre uma pessoa ou coisa, sem começar uma frase nova. Para pessoas, usamos who: "the champion who won the race". Para coisas, usamos that (ou which): "the stage that was the hottest".

Quando a informação é só um extra (não essencial para identificar quem/o quê), colocamos vírgulas ao redor: "Alaphilippe, who became a national hero in 2019, was dropped…". Sem a informação extra, a frase ainda faz sentido: "Alaphilippe was dropped…".
examples: in an edition of the Tour <mark>that</mark> has also been one of the hottest.
the former world road race champion, <mark>who became a national hero when he held the yellow jersey for 14 days in the 2019 Tour</mark>, was dropped
The rider <mark>who</mark> won the stage is Norwegian.
This is the bottle <mark>that</mark> Pogacar almost hit.

exercises.q: Which sentence uses a relative clause correctly?
options: The rider crashed, he was tired.
The rider who crashed was tired.
The rider whom crashed was tired.
exercises.explain_pt: “Who” liga a informação extra ao sujeito (pessoa) sem precisar de uma frase nova. “Whom” seria usado só se “rider” fosse objeto da oração, não sujeito.

exercises.q: Complete: “Pogacar, ___ has won the Tour many times, is very strong this year.”
exercises.explain_pt: “Pogacar” é uma pessoa, então usamos “who”. “Which” é para coisas, “whose” indica posse, “when” indica tempo.

exercises.q: Choose the correct relative pronoun: “This is the bottle ___ Pogacar ran over.”
exercises.explain_pt: “Bottle” é uma coisa, então usamos “which” (ou “that”). “Who”/“whom” são para pessoas; “whose” indica posse.

exercises.q: Complete com o pronome relativo para coisas: “an edition of the Tour ___ has also been one of the hottest.”
exercises.after: has also been one of the hottest.
exercises.hint_pt: pronome relativo para coisas (também pode ser 'which').

exercises.q: Complete com o pronome relativo para pessoas: “the champion, ___ became a national hero, was dropped on the climb.”
exercises.after: became a national hero, was dropped on the climb.
